question: Implement a custom `MyString` class that dynamically allocates memory for a C-style string. Provide a deep copy constructor and a deep copy assignment operator (`operator=`). Demonstrate how these are crucial to avoid shallow copies and dangling pointers when copying `MyString` objects.

test_cases.expected:
--- MyString Demonstration (Correct Version) ---
s1: "Hello" (Address of data: 0x...)
s2 (copy of s1): "Hello" (Address of data: 0x...)
s3 (default): "" (Address of data: 0x...)
s3 (assigned s1): "Hello" (Address of data: 0x...)

--- Modifying s1 ---
s1 (assigned temp 'World'): "World" (Address of data: 0x...)
s2 (should still be 'Hello'): "Hello" (Address of data: 0x...)
s3 (should still be 'Hello'): "Hello" (Address of data: 0x...)

--- Self-assignment test ---
s2 (after self-assignment): "Hello" (Address of data: 0x...)

--- Assignment from temporary ---
s3 (assigned temporary): "Temporary" (Address of data: 0x...)

--- Objects going out of scope ---
local_s: "Local String" (Address of data: 0x...)
local_copy: "Local String" (Address of data: 0x...)
Local strings went out of scope.
--- End of Demonstration ---

driver: int main() { MyString s1("Hello"); MyString s2 = s1; MyString s3; s3 = s1; MyString temp("World"); s1 = temp; s2 = s2; s3 = MyString("Temporary"); { MyString local_s("Local String"); MyString local_copy = local_s; } return 0; }
